lesson.menu_title: Talking About the Past -- My Story
lesson.menu_desc: Telling your own story and trajectory in the past simple -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- Talking About the Past
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 4 -- Talking About the Past

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a
the time in your life when you were a child
b
to change from a child into an adult over the years
c
your education, family history, and past experience
d
to finish your studies and receive a diploma
e
the series of jobs you have over the years in one field
f
to move to a new city or country to live or work
g
an important event or stage in your life
h
a moment when an important change begins
i
to succeed in doing something after a lot of effort
j
a difficult task that tests your ability
k
to succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty
l
something from the past that you remember

paras: Elena grew up in a small town in the mountains. Her childhood was simple and happy, but her family did not have much money. Her parents worked hard, and they always talked about the value of education. That background shaped the person she became. From an early age, Elena dreamed of a bigger life.

When she was eighteen, Elena moved to the capital to study. She graduated from university five years later, and that day was an important milestone. Her first job was a real challenge, because she knew almost no one in the city. She faced many difficulties, but slowly she overcame them. Little by little, she built a career she was proud of.

The real turning point came when a company abroad offered her a job. She decided to relocate, even though she felt afraid. The first year was hard, and she often missed home. In the end, she achieved her dream and never regretted the choice. Today, that brave decision is her favorite memory.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- B1 reading, a short life story

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: The text explains, step by step, how to find a job in a foreign country.
Elena worked hard, overcame many challenges, and achieved her dream through her life journey.
The text says that people should never leave their hometown.

items: Elena grew up in a big city by the sea.
The text says: 'Elena grew up in a small town in the mountains.'
Graduating from university was an important milestone for Elena.
The text says: 'she graduated from university... that day was an important milestone.'
Elena's first job was easy because she had many friends in the city.
The text says: 'Her first job was a real challenge, because she knew almost no one.'
Elena felt afraid when she decided to relocate abroad.
The text says: 'She decided to relocate, even though she felt afraid.'

gapfill.text: The past simple describes finished actions in the past. Regular verbs end in -ed (worked, moved, studied). Many common verbs are irregular (go -> went, be -> was/were, grow -> grew). In negatives and questions, use did / did not + the base verb: I did not go. Did you go?

bank.label: Useful sentences to tell your story
items: I grew up in a small town in the countryside.
My childhood was happy and simple.
My first job was a real challenge.
I relocated to another city for work.
The turning point came when I changed careers.
I faced many difficulties, but I overcame them.
Achieving that goal is my favorite memory.

lf.title: Regular or irregular past?
items: in a hospital for ten years.

lf.text: Notice: regular verbs add -ed (worked, moved). Irregular verbs change their form (grow -> grew, become -> became). There is no rule for irregular verbs -- you learn them with practice.

items.situation: Where did you grow up? Tell me about your hometown.
What did you study, and when did you graduate?
What was your first job? How was it?
tips: It was a real challenge because ...

items.situation: Tell me about a turning point in your life.
What is a challenge you overcame? How did you do it?

items: Tell your story in three sentences: where you grew up, what you studied, and your first job. Use the past simple.
Now describe one turning point or milestone in your life, and how you felt at that moment.
Tell your whole story, from childhood to today, with no notes. Use at least five past simple verbs.
A new colleague asks: 'So, how did you end up here?' Answer with a short version of your story.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model story
list: Model: 'I grew up in a small town in the south of Brazil. My childhood was happy, and my family always valued education. I studied medicine and graduated in 2010. My first job was a real challenge, but I overcame the difficulties and built a career I was proud of. The turning point came when I decided to relocate abroad. It was hard, but I achieved my dream, and that is my favorite memory.'
Regular past: worked, studied, moved, graduated, decided, relocated (verb + -ed).
Irregular past: was / were, grew, became, went, had, met, felt, made, took, left.
Negatives and questions use did: I did not study law. Where did you grow up?
answerkey.note: The past simple is for finished actions in the past. Regular verbs add -ed; irregular verbs change form. For negatives and questions, use did / did not + the base verb.

listenings.text: I still remember my first day at work, many years ago. I grew up in a small town, and I never imagined I would live in a big city. After I graduated, I moved to the capital and started my career. The first months were a real challenge. I knew almost no one, and I often felt alone. But little by little, I overcame my fears and made good friends. Looking back, that difficult year was an important turning point. It made me the person I am today.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah. I was thinking about our conversation yesterday, about big life decisions. Ten years ago, I made the biggest one of my life. I left my job and relocated to another country. Honestly, at first it was terrible. I missed my family, and everything felt strange. But I decided to stay and finish what I started. In the end, I achieved everything I wanted. It was the best decision I ever made. Call me later, I want to hear your story too. Bye!

extra_audio.text: When I was a child, I grew up in a small town. I studied hard and graduated from university. My first job was a challenge, but I overcame it. A few years later, I relocated to a new city. In the end, I achieved my dream.
